headlineOptions: Engineering Scalable Systems That Don't Break Under Pressure.
Full Stack Systems. Cloud Native. Built for Scale.
From UI to Infrastructure — I Ship Production-Ready Systems.
Turning Complex Requirements Into Clean, Scalable Architecture.
Building High-Impact Web Platforms With Precision.
hero.selectedHeadline: Engineering Scalable Systems That Don't Break Under Pressure.
hero.subtitle: Senior Full Stack Developer with 5+ years of experience building scalable web platforms using React, Next.js, Node.js, and AWS. I specialize in performance optimization, system architecture, and leading engineering teams.

about.title: About Me
about.description: I'm a results-driven Full Stack Developer with 5+ years of experience designing and building scalable web applications. My expertise spans frontend architecture, backend systems, cloud infrastructure, and DevOps automation. I focus on writing maintainable code, optimizing performance, and building systems that scale reliably in production.
highlights: Architected and deployed containerized applications on AWS ECS with Fargate
Built Next.js platform serving 100K+ monthly users
Designed microservices architectures for scalable applications
Led and mentored a team of 12 developers
Optimized CI/CD pipelines reducing deployment time by 40%

achievements: Developed and optimized a Next.js e-commerce platform serving 100K+ monthly users
Integrated WhatsApp Business API for automated customer support
Deployed containerized applications on AWS ECS with Fargate
Led a team of 12 developers and enforced engineering best practices
Implemented AWS Lambda serverless functions for real-time processing
Built a custom logging system with log rotation for production monitoring
Automated CI/CD using GitHub Actions reducing deployment time by 40%

projects.name: Yichi
projects.description: Interactive language learning platform for Chinese learners with dynamic lesson modules.
highlights: Integrated APIs for dynamic lesson rendering

detail.architecture: React handled the learner experience while Spring Boot powered lesson APIs, user progress, and adaptive content delivery.
decisions: Chose component-driven lesson blocks so new activity types could be shipped without refactoring the entire interface.
Used API-driven content rendering to keep lesson updates decoupled from frontend deployments.
Focused on responsive interaction patterns because users spent long sessions on tablets and smaller laptops.
challenges: Balancing rich interactions with performance on long lesson pages.
Keeping lesson state predictable while users moved between exercises quickly.
Reducing friction for content editors who needed frequent curriculum updates.
flow: Learner selects a lesson path.
Frontend requests lesson modules and progress state.
Backend assembles lesson content and returns activity configuration.
Progress events are persisted so the next session resumes cleanly.
outcomes: Created a modular lesson experience that could scale with new content formats.
Improved the maintainability of the learning UI by standardizing activity rendering.
Made curriculum delivery faster for the team shipping new lessons.

projects.name: Talitrix
projects.description: Offender tracking and monitoring platform with real-time dashboards.

detail.architecture: React dashboards consumed Python-backed monitoring services that aggregated tracking signals and surfaced live updates for operators.
decisions: Prioritized dashboard clarity over ornamental UI because operators needed fast signal recognition.
Split the interface into reusable monitoring widgets so views could be reconfigured by workflow.
Used streaming-friendly update patterns to keep critical panels fresh without full-page reloads.
challenges: Presenting high-volume activity data without overwhelming users.
Designing layouts that remained readable during live updates.
Keeping front-end performance stable when many widgets refreshed together.
flow: Tracking services ingest events.
Backend normalizes and exposes dashboard-ready data.
Frontend renders operator views with live status indicators.
Users drill into entities and review exception states.
outcomes: Delivered dashboards that supported faster operator response.
Improved readability of live monitoring screens.
Established a reusable pattern for real-time admin interfaces.

projects.name: Blox
projects.description: Real estate web platform for property listing and management.
highlights: Improved UX and mentored junior developers

detail.architecture: Next.js delivered a performant listing experience while Laravel powered property management, search data, and admin operations.
decisions: Used Next.js for routing and rendering performance on marketing and listing pages.
Designed responsive listing cards and filters around mobile-first browsing behavior.
Established reusable page and form patterns so the team could iterate faster.
challenges: Maintaining search usability across large listing sets.
Balancing marketing polish with the operational needs of admins.
Coordinating frontend consistency across a growing team.
flow: Visitors browse and filter listings.
Search and property data are served from Laravel APIs.
Agents manage listings through admin workflows.
Content and listing state stay synchronized across public and internal views.
outcomes: Shipped a faster and more consistent browsing experience.
Improved UI quality across responsive breakpoints.
Raised delivery quality through mentorship and pattern reuse.

projects.name: Accreda
projects.description: Educational platform for online learning and certifications.

detail.architecture: React handled course and certification experiences while Node.js services supported enrollment, content, and issuance workflows.
decisions: Created reusable course UI primitives to support multiple education flows.
Separated service concerns so enrollment and certification logic could evolve independently.
Optimized for maintainability because product requirements changed frequently.
challenges: Keeping the certification flow easy to follow for users.
Managing cross-service dependencies without creating brittle frontend assumptions.
Building components that worked for both learner-facing and admin-facing experiences.
flow: Learners enroll in courses.
Node.js services coordinate lessons, assessments, and completion status.
Frontend surfaces progress and certification milestones.
Completed learners receive certification-ready outcomes.
outcomes: Improved reuse across the platform UI.
Made the education flow easier to extend for future products.
Reduced frontend duplication through scalable component design.

projects.name: Contour
projects.description: Education portal with student and admin dashboards.

detail.architecture: React dashboards communicated with Node.js services hosted on AWS, with role-aware data shaping for student and admin experiences.
decisions: Implemented role-based UI boundaries early to prevent privilege leakage.
Used AWS-hosted APIs with secure integration patterns for operational reliability.
Kept dashboard modules isolated so admin and student flows could evolve independently.
challenges: Ensuring authorization remained consistent across multiple views.
Keeping dashboard performance strong as feature complexity increased.
Designing admin tooling that remained approachable for non-technical users.
flow: Users authenticate and receive role-scoped access.
Node.js services fetch and shape the relevant educational data.
AWS-hosted APIs serve dashboards and activity records.
Frontend renders task-specific views for students and admins.
outcomes: Delivered a more secure and maintainable role-aware portal.
Improved trust in permission-sensitive workflows.
Created a cleaner structure for future dashboard expansion.

projects.name: PMI
projects.description: Marketing rewards application with user activity tracking.

detail.architecture: React powered engagement flows while Node.js and AWS-backed services tracked actions, points, and leaderboard standings.
decisions: Separated reward calculation concerns from presentation logic so business rules stayed maintainable.
Designed leaderboard views for quick comprehension of rank and progress.
Kept the tracking engine modular to support changing campaign logic.
challenges: Making the reward system transparent enough for users to trust it.
Managing action tracking accuracy across campaign events.
Balancing gamification with performance and reporting needs.
flow: Users complete trackable marketing actions.
Backend services evaluate points and update standings.
Leaderboard and rewards data are returned to the frontend.
Users monitor progress and incentives in real time.
outcomes: Created a flexible rewards workflow that could support campaign changes.
Improved clarity of ranking and point progress for users.
Strengthened the product's engagement loop with measurable actions.

projects.name: Altius Hub
projects.description: Medicine distributor management platform with logistics dashboards.

detail.architecture: React dashboards consumed Python services and AWS infrastructure to surface inventory, movement, and logistics insights for distributor operations.
decisions: Organized the UI around operational visibility so users could spot supply issues quickly.
Optimized data-heavy views for performance because dashboard lag directly affected workflow efficiency.
Used AWS-hosted infrastructure patterns that supported dependable reporting and scaling.
challenges: Managing large, fast-changing operational datasets.
Keeping dashboards readable while exposing enough detail for logistics decisions.
Reducing performance bottlenecks in dashboard rendering.
flow: Operational data is collected from inventory and logistics sources.
Python services aggregate and normalize key metrics.
AWS-backed infrastructure serves dashboard-ready responses.
Frontend displays inventory health, distribution status, and logistics views.
outcomes: Improved logistics visibility for distributor workflows.
Reduced rendering friction in data-heavy views.
Created a stronger operational dashboard foundation for scale.

howIBuild.title: I build with product clarity, system depth, and AI-assisted speed.
howIBuild.intro: My workflow blends strong engineering fundamentals with AI-first execution. I use AI to accelerate exploration, drafting, and debugging, but I keep architecture, review quality, and production reliability grounded in deliberate engineering decisions.

pillars.title: System Thinking First
pillars.description: Before shipping UI, I define boundaries, state flow, and failure modes so the product can scale without being rewritten every sprint.
points: Break features into clean frontend, service, and infrastructure concerns.
Model data flow early so edge cases surface before implementation.
Design for maintainability, not just delivery speed.

pillars.title: AI As A Force Multiplier
pillars.description: I use AI for faster ideation, code scaffolding, debugging support, and documentation, while keeping human judgment on architecture and final quality.
points: Use AI to generate first-pass implementation paths and compare approaches quickly.
Pressure-test prompts with constraints so outputs are useful, not generic.
Treat AI as a collaborator in delivery, never as a replacement for engineering review.

pillars.title: Production-Ready Delivery
pillars.description: I care about the last 20% of work: observability, performance, deployability, and making sure the product survives real user traffic.
points: Prioritize performance budgets and rendering efficiency.
Keep CI/CD and deployment workflows part of the feature conversation.
Document decisions so teams can extend the system without guesswork.

workflow.title: Frame the problem
workflow.description: Clarify user goals, system boundaries, and the minimum reliable architecture.

workflow.title: Prototype with AI
workflow.description: Use AI to accelerate drafts, compare implementation options, and expose blind spots early.

workflow.title: Build the core path
workflow.description: Ship the happy path with maintainable components, data flow, and operational hooks.

workflow.title: Harden for production
workflow.description: Review edge cases, performance, deployment readiness, and handoff quality.

contact.title: Let's Build Something Meaningful
contact.description: If you're building a product that needs solid architecture, clean frontend systems, and scalable backend infrastructure — let's talk.

ai.title: Ask About Me
suggestedQuestions: How do you use AI in your workflow?
Which project best shows your system design skills?
ai.systemPrompt: You are Jeet's portfolio AI assistant. Answer with a confident, engineering-focused tone. Keep answers concise, clear, and grounded in the provided portfolio context. Highlight system design, technical tradeoffs, delivery thinking, and the actual technologies mentioned in the portfolio data.
